description: A vertical designed recruitment/promotional poster. The image is a color portrait photograph of a young woman, smiling slightly at the camera, wearing a bright blue hard hat, a neon yellow-green high-visibility safety vest over a navy jacket, with a braided ponytail; she stands outdoors at what appears to be a construction or solar-array site (blurred steel framework in the background). A large semi-transparent white line-art seal of the U.S. Department of Labor ('Department of Labor / United States of America') is watermarked centrally over the lower portion. A dark blue gradient frames the bottom, where white italic serif typography reads 'APPRENTICESHIP WORKS' in large letters, with a smaller line beneath: 'Explore opportunities at apprenticeship.gov' (note: 'opportunities' is misspelled 'opportunities/opportunities'). The clean, aspirational design markets skilled-trade apprenticeships—notably featuring a young woman to broaden appeal—and provides a website call-to-action. Target audience: young people (including women) considering trades/apprenticeship pathways.
notable_text: APPRENTICESHIP WORKS; Explore opportunities at apprenticeship.gov
